framing: Air Force avionics officer transitioning to defense contractor flight systems role

jd.title: Senior Flight Systems Engineer
jd.style_note: Defense-cleared: security clearance mandatory, ITAR-controlled, specific military platform experience valued

requirements.text: 10+ years of experience in avionics systems engineering, flight test, or aircraft modification programmes
Experience with DO-178C (airborne software) and DO-254 (airborne hardware) certification processes
Knowledge of MIL-STD-1553 data bus architecture and ARINC 429 communication protocols
Experience with flight test instrumentation (FTI) and telemetry systems
Familiarity with F-35, F-16, or C-130 avionics systems
Experience with Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) using Cameo Systems Modeler or IBM Rhapsody
PE license or INCOSE CSEP certification

responsibilities: Lead avionics integration and flight test activities for military aircraft modification programmes
Develop system requirements and interface control documents (ICDs) for avionics subsystems
Coordinate DO-178C/DO-254 certification activities with FAA/DCMA designated engineering representatives
Support flight test planning, execution, and data analysis for modified military aircraft
Interface with USAF Programme Offices (AFLCMC) on technical reviews and milestone decisions

expected_output.summary: US Air Force officer (Major, separating) with 12 years of avionics engineering and flight test experience. Currently Avionics Flight Commander at Edwards AFB, 412th Test Wing, leading flight test of F-16 avionics upgrades. Previously avionics engineer on C-130J sustainment programme at Warner Robins ALC. Active TS/SCI clearance. Experience with MIL-STD-1553, DO-178C certification coordination, and flight test instrumentation. MS Electrical Engineering from AFIT. Transitioning to defense industry.

experience.company: US Air Force — 412th Test Wing, Edwards AFB
experience.title: Avionics Flight Commander (Major, O-4)
bullets: Led 18-person flight test team executing F-16 Block 70 avionics upgrade developmental testing
Managed flight test instrumentation (FTI) installation and telemetry system configuration for 6 test aircraft
Coordinated MIL-STD-1553 data bus integration testing between radar (AN/APG-83 AESA), EW suite, and mission computer
Reviewed and approved test plans, test cards, and safety review board packages for 120+ test sorties
Interfaced with AFLCMC Programme Office on technical baseline reviews and deficiency report adjudication

experience.company: US Air Force — Warner Robins Air Logistics Complex (ALC)
experience.title: Avionics Systems Engineer (Captain, O-3)
bullets: Managed C-130J avionics sustainment engineering for fleet of 130+ aircraft across Air Mobility Command
Coordinated DO-178C certification activities for 3 software updates to C-130J mission computer
Developed engineering investigation reports for 12 avionics-related mishap/incident investigations
Led ARINC 429 interface analysis for CNS/ATM upgrade (ADS-B Out compliance) across C-130J fleet

experience.company: US Air Force — Undergraduate Pilot Training (UPT) Selection, Sheppard AFB
experience.title: Avionics Maintenance Officer (1st Lieutenant, O-2)
bullets: Led avionics maintenance section (45 enlisted technicians) for T-38C trainer aircraft fleet
Managed avionics component repair and calibration for radar altimeter, TACAN, and UHF/VHF systems
Achieved 94% mission capable rate for assigned aircraft, exceeding Air Education and Training Command standard

certifications: USAF Test and Evaluation certification — Air Force Test Center (2022)
Acquisition Professional Development Program (APDP) Level II — Engineering (2019)
education: MS Electrical Engineering — Air Force Institute of Technology (AFIT) (2016-2018)
BS Electrical and Computer Engineering — US Air Force Academy (2010-2014)

jd_requirements: 10+ years avionics systems engineering flight test aircraft modification
DO-178C airborne software DO-254 airborne hardware certification
MBSE Cameo Systems Modeler IBM Rhapsody
deliberate_flaws: No DO-254 (airborne hardware) experience — only DO-178C (software) coordination
No MBSE experience — no Cameo Systems Modeler or IBM Rhapsody
No PE license or INCOSE CSEP certification
No F-35 experience (JD lists F-35 as preferred)
DO-178C experience is coordination/oversight, not hands-on certification engineering
No ICD development experience — reviewed ICDs, didn't author them
Military officer transitioning to industry — different procurement culture, contractor dynamics
No commercial aviation experience (all military platforms)
No systems requirements development using formal tools (DOORS, Jama) — used military formats
Flight test experience is developmental test (DT), not operational test (OT) — different perspective

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
